title: [YouTube] Fix 403 error and throttling
body: Decrypting `signatureCipher` to fix the recent 403 error, as well as to solve the long-standing download speed throttling issue (#2950). This was made possible thanks to the lightweight JavaScript interpreter [dukpy](https://pypi.org/project/dukpy/).

files_modified.patch:
@@ -3,6 +3,8 @@
 from ..common import *
 from ..extractor import VideoExtractor
 
+import dukpy
+from urllib.parse import urlparse, parse_qs, urlencode
 from xml.dom.minidom import parseString
 
 class YouTube(VideoExtractor):
@@ -68,45 +70,32 @@ class YouTube(VideoExtractor):
          'audio_encoding': 'AAC', 'audio_bitrate': '24'},
     ]
 
+    def dethrottle(js, url):
+        def n_to_n(js, n):
+            # Examples:
+            #   yma - https://www.youtube.com/s/player/84314bef/player_ias.vflset/en_US/base.js
+            #   Xka - https://www.youtube.com/s/player/dc0c6770/player_ias.vflset/sv_SE/base.js
+            f1 = match1(js, r'a\.set\("n",b\),[$\w]+\.length\|\|([$\w]+)\(""\)')
+            f1def = match1(js, r'\W%s=(function\(\w+\).+?\)});' % re.escape(f1))
+            n = dukpy.evaljs('(%s)("%s")' % (f1def, n))
+            return n
+
+        u = urlparse(url)
+        qs = parse_qs(u.query)
+        n = n_to_n(js, qs['n'][0])
+        qs['n'] = [n]
+        return u._replace(query=urlencode(qs, doseq=True)).geturl()
+

         # Examples:

-            code = re.sub(r'\{', r': ', code)
-            code = re.sub(r'\}', r'\n', code)
-            code = re.sub(r'var\s+', r'', code)
-            code = re.sub(r'(\w+).join\(""\)', r'"".join(\1)', code)
-            code = re.sub(r'(\w+).length', r'len(\1)', code)
-            code = re.sub(r'(\w+).slice\((\w+)\)', r'\1[\2:]', code)
-            code = re.sub(r'(\w+).splice\((\w+),(\w+)\)', r'del \1[\2:\2+\3]', code)
-            code = re.sub(r'(\w+).split\(""\)', r'list(\1)', code)
-            return code

-            match1(js, r'"signature",([$\w]+)\(\w+\.\w+\)') or \
-            match1(js, r'=([$\w]+)\(decodeURIComponent\(')
-        f1def = match1(js, r'function %s(\(\w+\)\{[^\{]+\})' % re.escape(f1)) or \
-                match1(js, r'\W%s=function(\(\w+\)\{[^\{]+\})' % re.escape(f1))
-        f1def = re.sub(r'([$\w]+\.)([$\w]+\(\w+,\d+\))', r'\2', f1def)
+        #   BPa - https://www.youtube.com/s/player/84314bef/player_ias.vflset/en_US/base.js
+        #   Xva - https://www.youtube.com/s/player/dc0c6770/player_ias.vflset/sv_SE/base.js

+        f1 = match1(js, r'=([$\w]+)\(decodeURIComponent\(')
+        f1def = match1(js, r'\W%s=function(\(\w+\)\{[^\{]+\})' % re.escape(f1))
+        f1def = re.sub(r'([$\w]+\.)([$\w]+\(\w+,\d+\))', r'\2', f1def)  # remove . prefix
         f1def = 'function %s%s' % (f1, f1def)
-        code = tr_js(f1def)
-        f2s = set(re.findall(r'([$\w]+)\(\w+,\d+\)', f1def))
+        f2s = set(re.findall(r'([$\w]+)\(\w+,\d+\)', f1def))  # find all invoked function names
         for f2 in f2s:
             f2e = re.escape(f2)
             f2def = re.search(r'[^$\w]%s:function\((\w+,\w+)\)(\{[^\{\}]+\})' % f2e, js)
@@ -115,13 +104,10 @@ def tr_js(code):
             else:
                 f2def = re.search(r'[^$\w]%s:function\((\w+)\)(\{[^\{\}]+\})' % f2e, js)
                 f2def = 'function {}({},b){}'.format(f2e, f2def.group(1), f2def.group(2))
-            f2 = re.sub(r'\$', '_dollar', f2)  # replace dollar sign
-            code = code + 'global _sig_%s\n' % f2 + tr_js(f2def)
-
-        f1 = re.sub(r'\$', '_dollar', f1)  # replace dollar sign
-        code = code + '_sig=_sig_%s(s)' % f1
-        exec(code, globals(), locals())
-        return locals()['_sig']
+            js_code += f2def + ';'
+        js_code += f1def + ';%s("%s")' % (f1, s)
+        sig = dukpy.evaljs(js_code)
+        return sig

steps.operation: Add a new runtime dependency on dukpy for JavaScript evaluation
steps.reason: The extractor now executes small snippets of YouTube’s player JavaScript to decrypt `signatureCipher` and rewrite the throttling `n` parameter; this requires bundling a lightweight JS interpreter (`dukpy`) as an installation dependency.
steps.side_effects: Packaging/installation behavior changes for all users (new dependency to resolve); downstream distro packaging may need updates; environments without dukpy wheels may require build toolchains.

steps.operation: Introduce URL query parsing imports and implement `dethrottle(js, url)` to rewrite YouTube’s `n` parameter via dukpy
steps.reason: YouTube uses an obfuscated `n` query parameter to enforce download throttling. The fix extracts the relevant transformation function from `base.js`, evaluates it with dukpy, and rewrites the URL so downloads are no longer speed-limited.
steps.side_effects: All generated stream URLs are mutated (query string changes); failures in regex extraction or JS evaluation can break URL generation; increased network+CPU cost due to fetching and evaluating player JS.

steps.operation: Refactor signature deciphering to evaluate extracted player functions directly in JavaScript instead of translating JS to Python
steps.reason: The prior approach attempted to transliterate YouTube’s evolving JavaScript into Python, which is brittle and broke with recent player updates (causing 403). Executing the extracted functions with dukpy is more robust and fixes `signatureCipher` decryption for valid signed URLs.
steps.side_effects: Signature generation now depends on dukpy correctness and JS extraction regexes; subtle changes in YouTube player code may require regex updates; potential performance impact from evaluating JS per signature.

steps.operation: Rewrite `prepare()` to extract `ytInitialPlayerResponse` from the watch page, validate `playabilityStatus`, parse `signatureCipher`, and apply dethrottling to both formats and adaptiveFormats while removing legacy/dead branches
steps.reason: To fix 403 and throttling consistently, the extractor now: (1) loads the watch page, (2) finds and downloads `base.js`, (3) parses `ytInitialPlayerResponse` as the single source of truth, (4) errors early with guidance to use cookies when playability is restricted, (5) builds stream URLs by decrypting `signatureCipher` and rewriting throttled `n` parameters, and (6) simplifies/removes outdated `get_video_info`/DASH parsing code paths.
steps.side_effects: Different failure modes and user messaging (more direct cookie requirement); live/edge cases handled by old branches may regress; more reliance on watch page structure and `ytInitialPlayerResponse` regex; DASH/adaptive stream handling changes may affect format availability/order.

steps.operation: Simplify `extract()` to use URLs precomputed in `prepare()` (remove redundant sig/s handling and on-demand JS fetching)
steps.reason: `prepare()` now fully resolves final playable URLs (including signature decipher and dethrottling). Keeping signature appending logic in `extract()` becomes dead/incorrect and could double-append or fetch JS unnecessarily.
steps.side_effects: Any callers relying on `extract()` to patch URLs post-prepare will no longer get that behavior; if `prepare()` misses a case, `extract()` won’t compensate; download URL generation is now centralized.

steps.operation: Update the YouTube test case to use a new video URL for info-only extraction
steps.reason: The previous commented-out/old test URL no longer reliably exercises the updated signatureCipher/throttling logic. The test is adjusted to actively run against a known working watch URL to validate the new extraction path.
steps.side_effects: Test suite becomes dependent on the availability/playability of the chosen public YouTube video and YouTube’s current page/player layout; potential for flaky CI if the video is removed/geo-restricted.

step_plan.summary: This PR fixes YouTube 403 download failures and resolves long-standing throttling by decrypting `signatureCipher` and rewriting throttling parameters using `dukpy` to execute extracted player JavaScript. It also simplifies the extractor to rely on `ytInitialPlayerResponse`, improves playability/cookies handling, and updates tests accordingly.
